title: eupatorium rotundifolium var. pubescens
intro: Eupatorium rotundifolium var. pubescens, also known as the hairy boneset, is a plant species in the Eupatorium genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The hairy boneset has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. It is believed to offer health benefits such as relief from muscle and joint pain, as well as reduced inflammation and fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the hairy boneset are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
